raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a formal complaint against Citibank for violating my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), 15 U.S.C. 1666i, regarding a dispute for unsatisfactory services and breach of contract. 

I have been a loyal Citibank cardholder for over 20 years. On XX/XX/year>, a plumbing merchant was dispatched through my home warranty company to clear an emergency sewer backup. The plumber failed to resolve the backup. To excuse his incomplete work, the plumber claimed the backup persisted because my septic tank was completely full. The plumber charged me {$45.00} for the service call and {$210.00} to snake the sewer line. The very next day, I paid a septic system company to inspect the tank. They confirmed the tank was NOT full and was not causing the issue. Instead, they ran a diagnostic camera through the lines and proved the original sewer blockage was still fully intact. The septic company then snaked the line and successfully cleared the blockage, immediately resolving the backup. 

I opened a timely dispute online with Citibank and provided this documentation ( attached/uploaded ). Citibank summarily closed the dispute in favor of the merchant. 

When I called to appeal, a Citibank supervisor stated that the bank " can not force the merchant to accept the chargeback '' and is " not empowered to take the money back. '' This is a direct misrepresentation of federal law and card network rules regarding " Services Not as Described. " Because Citibank 's frontline staff closed my valid case and provided legally incorrect information, I am filing this CFPB complaint immediately to prevent the expiration of my chargeback rights due to bank error. I request that Citibank reopen my case, review the attached camera evidence and invoices, and permanently credit my account. 

I am including a PDF of my request of service with the Warranty company that dispatched the plumbing service, and the invoice from the septic company that cleared the sewer line the next day indicting that it was not a full septic but a clogged sewer line. I also include the letter from Citibank closing the case indicating that they aasked the merchant and he would not honor the refund.
